callouts.text:
In the Forward Pass: It serves a pragmatic purpose—flipping weight matrices so they align with incoming data shapes.

In the Backward Pass: It serves a mathematical purpose—flipping the directional mapping of weights and activations so gradients can be distributed correctly to earlier layers.

In Attention Layers: It serves an algorithmic purpose—allowing a matrix to be multiplied by itself to calculate global token-to-token relationships.

Inverse ($W^{-1}$): Tries to perfectly reconstruct the past. It fails in neural networks because of rectangular shapes, ReLU data loss, and extreme computational cost.Transpose ($W^T$): Tries to assign blame for the current error. It is computationally instant, handles any matrix shape, and provides the exact directional vectors needed for gradient descent.

It's for class, random.

and np.random.randn(2,3) is for np
